questions.question: In the following questions, a sentence has been given in Direct/ Indirect speech. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Direct/ Indirect speech. "Please let me go back to my work," Parul said to me.
options: Parul requested me to let her go back to her work.
Parul requested me to please let her go back to her work.
Parul requested me to let me go back to my work.
Parul requested me to let her going back to her work.
questions.explanation: The use of the word 'please' is incorrect when used with 'requested'. The first person possessive pronoun 'my' will change to the third person possessive 'her'. The base form of the verb 'go' should be used with the modal verb 'let'. A is the right answer.

questions.question: upon the _______ contained
questions.explanation: 'Essential', 'principal'- out of context, as they are adjectives while we need a noun. 'Fundamentalist'- does not make sense here as it is used to refer to a person. 'Principle' refers to belief or fact. Hence, A is the correct answer.

questions.question: church-nation, _______ piety lies
questions.explanation: The word we are looking for, as per the context, is devotion/dutiful. 'Familiar' and 'compliance' (noun), which means adherence, are out of context. Lovingly- adverb (we are looking for an adjective to qualify 'piety'). B is the correct answer.

questions.question: birds _______ the fields
questions.explanation: 'Dumped', 'isolated', 'segregated' do not make sense here, as neither of them refer to an action that can be done by birds. Weeded- to remove unwanted plants from an area/ground.Other clues include the use of 'plough' and 'fields' in the sentence. C is the correct answer.

questions.question: they still ________ him
questions.explanation: As per the context, the word should be of negative connotation (the blank is the preceded by 'still' and before that there is a statement with positive tone). Abetted, which means to encourage something wrong, is exactly opposite of what we are looking for. Ill treatment- wrong form of the phrase, as this is a noun while we need a verb. A makes no sense here. Persecuted- subject to hostility and ill treatment. D is the correct answer.

questions.question: was miraculously ________.
questions.explanation: As per the context, the blank should be filled by a word of positive tone. So, A and D are ruled out. Safeguard- wrong form of the verb used; we need a past participle as the clause is in passive voice. Hence, C is the correct answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, four sentences are given, which when properly arranged, make up a paragraph. Rearrange the given sentences and choose the option that provides the correct final order. P. The day you stop feeling this way is the day you'll stop growing so learn to embrace this feeling and take it positively. Q. But over time, I've learnt that it's actually good to feel this way because it means you care and you want to be the best version of yourself. R. At first, these thoughts would really bring me down and demotivate me. S. I always have this feeling at the back of my mind that I'm not doing enough and not living up to my full potential.
questions.explanation: S can be the only opening statement as all others contain either pronouns or connectors which require something/someone to which they should relate to. R follows by referring to the thoughts mentioned in S. QP follow as a pair: Q contradicts R and tells us why it's good to feel this way. P follows by telling us what would happen if one stopped feeling this way. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, four sentences are given, which when properly arranged, make up a paragraph. Rearrange the given sentences and choose the option that provides the correct final order. P. I've been fortunate to get mostly positive comments on my YouTube videos. Q. But when it comes to plain mean, hate comments, I think it's best to just ignore them. R. Of course, constructive criticism is always good, and you should use that feedback to improve yourself. S. But there's the occasional hate comment, which can sometimes make you question yourself.
questions.explanation: Q, R and S start with structure words like 'but' and 'of course' and cannot be the opening statement in a paragraph. P begins the passage by telling us about positive comments received by the speaker. S follows by telling us of the occasional hate comment received by the speaker. RQ follow as a pair: R tells us of constructive criticism which is desirable, and Q tells us of hate comments which aren't. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following question, a sentence has been given in Active/Passive Voice. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive/Active Voice. The Vietnamese bought medium-sized shipments from Polish smugglers.
options: Medium-sized shipments were bought by the Vietnamese from the Polish smugglers.
Medium-sized shipments are bought by the Vietnamese from the Polish smugglers.
Medium-sized shipments had been bought by the Vietnamese from the Polish smugglers.
Medium-sized shipments were being bought by the Vietnamese from the Polish smugglers.
questions.explanation: The sentence is in active voice and in simple past tense (bought). B is incorrect as 'are bought' is in simple present tense. C is incorrect as 'had been bought' is in past perfect tense. D is incorrect as 'were being bought' is in past continuous tense. A is the right answer, as 'were bought' is in simple past tense.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, select the word closest in meaning to the word given. Reminisce
questions.explanation: 'Dangle' means hang or swing loosely. 'Randomize' means to employ random selection or sampling. 'Frugal' means sparing or economical with regards to money or food. 'Reminisce' means to indulge in enjoyable recollection of past events. Even 'recall' has the same meaning, making option B, the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, select the word closest in meaning to the word given. Loggerheads
questions.explanation: To be 'at loggerheads' with someone means to strongly 'disagree' with them. 'Aligned' means placed or arranged (things) in a straight line. Weighty - heavy. Straightforward - Uncomplicated and easy to do or understand. None of these convey the desired meaning. Hence, option A will be the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the word opposite in meaning to the word given. Obliterate
questions.explanation: 'Obliterate' means to destroy utterly; wipe out. The most obvious opposite would be 'create', making option B, the correct answer. Yield - produce or provide. Summon - Order (someone) to be present. Subsume - include or absorb (something) in something else. None of these convey the required meaning.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the word opposite in meaning to the word given. Subjugate
questions.explanation: 'Subjugate' means to bring under domination or control, especially by conquest. 'Liberate' means to set (someone) free from imprisonment, slavery, or oppression. The two are antonyms of each other, making option C, the correct answer. Promote - Support or actively encourage Scrap - to abolish or cancel. Satisfy - Meet the expectations, needs, or desires of (someone).

questions.question: In each of the following questions, a part/ phrase of the sentence has been bracketed. You need to identify which of the given options can both grammatically and contextually replace the bracketed part. In case the part is correct, mark option E as your answer. You can (count for) me for the support in any difficult situation.
questions.explanation: Count down- wait impatiently or excitedly for something to happen. Count against- affect negatively (Not having a university degree will count against her). Count on me (something)- to rely on/depend. Only C fits the context of the statement. Hence, C is the correct answer.

questions.question: In each of the following questions, a part/ phrase of the sentence has been bracketed. You need to identify which of the given options can both grammatically and contextually replace the bracketed part. In case the part is correct, mark option E as your answer. You need to (catch your breath) on the recent happenings.
questions.explanation: Catch your breath- to take a short rest after doing something physically difficult. Catch off-guard- to do so something people aren't expecting. Catch red-handed- to witness someone doing something they shouldn't be. Catch up on- become up-to-date; hence A is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the idiom/Phrase. Not a spark of decency
options: Not even the slightest bit of hope
questions.explanation: The idiom "Not a spark of decency" means ill mannered. Although A is close in meaning, but it refers to a person, while the idiom is only a descriptive quality.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the idiom/Phrase. Pay off old scores
questions.explanation: The idiom "Pay off old scores" refers to taking revenge.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the alternative which can best substitute the given phrase. The study of line of descents traced continuously from an ancestor
questions.explanation: The use of the fingers and hands to communicate and convey ideas = Dactylology. A stoppered glass container into which wine or spirit is decanted = Decanter. A collection of wild animals kept in captivity for exhibition = Menagerie. Hence, option A will be the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the alternative which can best substitute the given phrase. One who has escaped from captivity or is in hiding
questions.explanation: One who believes in fate = Fatalist. A lover of good food = Gourmand. A person of intellectual or erudite tastes = Highbrow. Hence, option B will be the correct answer.

questions.question: Some parts of the sentences have errors and some are correct. Find out which part of a sentence has an error choose the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, choose "No Error" option. My Mother exclaimed (1)/ how fast (2)/ the days had pass. (3)/ No error (4)
questions.explanation: The correct sentence is 'My Mother exclaimed how fast the days had passed'. The past perfect construction (had + verb) always uses the verb in the past participle form. C is the right answer.

questions.question: Some parts of the sentences have errors and some are correct. Find out which part of a sentence has an error. If a sentence is free from error, choose "No error" option. Its always a (1)/ beautiful day (2)/ in my neighborhood. (3)/ No Error (4)
questions.explanation: The contraction "it's" (it is) should be used in place of the possessive pronoun 'its', as the latter is used to show belonging (e.g. the dog wagged its tail).

questions.question: A sentence is given with a blank to be filled in an with appropriate word. Four alternatives are suggested. Choose the correct option. Even the slightest drop of poison can _______ a whole bottle of milk.
questions.explanation: Vitiate means to spoil or impair the quality of something. Emanate means to originate or radiate. Envisage means to contemplate or conceive or form a mental picture of something to be produced in future. Imbue means to inspire or permeate with. The appropriate word here is 'vitiate'.

questions.question: A sentence is given with a blank to be filled in with an appropriate word. Four alternatives are suggested. Choose the correct option. Instead of being careful, today's teenagers are trying to show their ____ while driving.
questions.explanation: Veracity means truthfulness. Creativity means having the ability to create. Modesty means the quality of being relatively moderate and limited. Temerity means excessive confidence or boldness or audacity. D is the most appropriate fit, according to the context.

questions.question: Four words are given in each question, out of which only one word is correctly spelled. Find the correctly spelled word.
questions.explanation: The correctly spelled word is "annihilate", which means to destroy.

questions.question: Four words are given in each question, but of which only one word is correctly spelled. Find out the correctly spelled word.
questions.explanation: The correctly spelled word is 'efflorescence' which refers to the stage of flowering in a plant.
